setup: 2 ordinary Lv 1.0 Infantry attacker Colonel Mueller with Gatot versus 1 FC1 Lv 1.0 Infantry plus 1 FC1 Lv 1.0 Marksman defender Alpaca with Gatot and Vulcanus.

turn_inference.cadence: King's Bestowal triggers each time Gatot's Infantry attacks
assumptions: Both sides' Gatot King's Bestowal rows show 90 triggers.
For these controlled Gatot Infantry tests, Martin treats King's Bestowal's trigger count as the exact battle-round count.

attacker.name: Colonel Mueller
attacker.troop_stat_reference: wos_sim/troop_catalog.py::_TIER_ROWS :: ordinary Infantry T1, no Fire Crystal

composition.troop_stat_reference: wos_sim/troop_catalog.py::_TIER_ROWS :: ordinary Infantry T1, no Fire Crystal

stats_capture.notes: Marksman Health row is cropped out of the pasted stat screenshot and is left null. Other visible stat rows were transcribed from the stat panel.

hero_skills.name: Golden Guard
hero_skills.effect: Gatot commands his troops with imperial guard tactics, increasing his Infantry's Defense by 6%.

hero_skills.name: King's Bestowal
hero_skills.effect: The great kings blesses Gatot's Infantry, granting Infantry a Shield with protection equal to Attack*6% each time they attack, for 1 turn.

troop_passives_active.reason: opponent fields Infantry and Marksman, not Lancers

defender.name: Alpaca
defender.kills_inference: inferred from attacker injured + lightly injured because defender participant kills are not visible in the pasted crop
defender.tier_display: FC1 Lv 1.0 Infantry + FC1 Lv 1.0 Marksman

stats_capture.notes: Marksman Health row is cropped out of the pasted stat screenshot and is left null. Right-side unit cards show both Infantry and Marksman as FC1 Lv 1.0.

hero_skills.name: Golden Guard
hero_skills.effect: Gatot commands his troops with imperial guard tactics, increasing his Infantry's Defense by 6%.

hero_skills.name: King's Bestowal
hero_skills.effect: The great kings blesses Gatot's Infantry, granting Infantry a Shield with protection equal to Attack*6% each time they attack, for 1 turn.

notes: Source screenshots were pasted directly into the Codex thread: overview, stat bonuses, and battle details.
Battle tile shown at top of overview: X:746 Y:475.
Left/red side attacker is Colonel Mueller at X:745 Y:473.
Right/blue side defender is Alpaca at X:747 Y:475.
Mueller fields 2 ordinary Lv 1.0 Infantry, not Fire Crystal.
Defender fields 1 FC1 Lv 1.0 Infantry and 1 FC1 Lv 1.0 Marksman; both right-side troop cards show the FC1 badge.
Both sides' Gatot King's Bestowal rows show 90 triggers.
Defender Vulcanus Marksman skill rows show 1, 30, and 30 triggers.
Skill kills are displayed as dashes and recorded as zero for visible skill rows.
Marksman Health stat row is not visible in the pasted stat screenshot and is intentionally stored as null for both sides.

_extraction.extracted_by: Codex local visual transcription with OCR-style structured capture
manual_overrides: Right-side defender normalized to Alpaca from the prior RFJ Alpaca reports.
Right-side Infantry and Marksman manually tagged as FC1 T1 because both visible troop cards show the red FC1 badge.
Vulcanus skill names not guessed; visible trigger counts are preserved with unidentified skill labels.

checks: attacker troops 2 = injured 1 + lightly injured 1 + survivors 0 + losses 0
defender troops 2 = injured 0 + lightly injured 0 + survivors 2 + losses 0
attacker power loss -3 is consistent with one ordinary T1 Infantry severe injury and one ordinary T1 Infantry light injury not counted in power loss
defender has no power loss and no injured/lost troops
missing: attacker Marksman Health stat row cropped out
defender Marksman Health stat row cropped out
defender participant kills not visible; inferred as 2

_stat_base_reference: STAT BASE REFERENCE: Right-side Alpaca troops are Fire-Crystal (FC) troops in this report. Use docs/TroopStats/WOS_Troop_Stats_FC1-FC10_T1-T10.json for FC1 T1 Infantry and FC1 T1 Marksman, not ordinary T1 assumptions. Mueller attacker Infantry is ordinary/no-FC T1 from wos_sim/troop_catalog.py::_TIER_ROWS.
